Notice Of Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery; Request For Further Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jonathan H. Bornstein, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Michelle Chodor

No other appearance stated. After hearing, tentative adopted as modified.
1. Defendant Fabiola Martinez-Esquer's Motion to dismiss for failure to comply with court order compelling discovery: off-calendar, based on plaintiff's representation that verifications have been provided.
2. Request for monetary sanctions is denied. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltfs Michelle Chodor And Douglas Mandler Attendance At A Deposition
Off Calendar Feb-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Fabiola Martinez-Esquer's motion to compel attendance at deposition is granted. No opposition has been filed. The parties shall schedule the depositions at a mutually agreeable time. The depositions shall be completed within 21 days of notice of this order. The request for monetary sanctions is denied as the declaration does not state any facts enabling the Court to set the amount of sanctions. Judge: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Pltfs' Responses To Deft'S Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Faviola Martinez-Esquer's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories and request for production of documents pursuant to CCP Section 2030.290 and 2031.300 is granted as the responses are unverified. Plaintiff shall serve verified responses within 15 days of notice of this order. There is no motion to compel further responses before the Court. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Apr-22-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FABIOLA MARTINEZ-ESQUER'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT.
APPEARANCES: JONATHAN H. BORNSTEIN, ESQUIRE (BORNSTEIN & BORNSTEIN, 415.409.7611) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JEANETTE N. LITTLE, ESQUIRE (PHILLIP M. ANDERSEN & ASSOCIATES, 415.3600) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED UPON CONDITION THAT DEFENDANT IS TO PAY $1,500.00 TO PLAINTIFF FOR COSTS AND ATTORNEY FEES. ANSWER TO BE FILED TODAY. MR. BORNSTEIN IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-27-2007 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up.
Off Calendar Dec-06-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Judge Ollie Marie-Victoire, Clerk: Sajja Ravinantapricha & Carla Amador, CSR: Karen Farnsworth CSR #5162 (Department 218)

Appearances
Plaintiff, Michelle Chodor and Douglas Mandler, are present.
Attorney, Jonathan Bornstein, is present representing the plaintiffs.

Defendant, Fabiola Martinez-Esguer, is not present.
(Default was entered on 10/30/07 as to the defendant.

This cause came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing. Michelle Chodor and Douglas Mandler are sworn and examined by Jonathan Bornstein.

After hearing the testimony and considering of the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendant, Fabiola Martinez-Esguer, as follows:

Defendant shall pay plaintiff, as and for damages, the sum of $19,945.65 and cost in the sum of $1,166.65 for a total Judgment of $21,112.30. Default Judgment by Court signed in open court by Judge Ollie Marie-Victoire.
